TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: naizua zazua ti sa Kului, a naputsekeɫ tua vuluvulung, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: naizua zazua ti sa kuɭui, a napuʦəkəʎ tua vuɭuvuɭuŋ, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: naizua zazua ti sa Kului, a napucekelj tua vuluvulung, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: naizua zazua ti sa kuɣui, a napuʦəkəʎ tua vuɣuvuɣuŋ, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: Once there was a person called Kului who was married to a hundred-pace snake, according to the story.

S.FORM: saka nu qezemezemetj tsautsau, saka nu maqadaw qatjuvi.
S.PHON: saka nu qəzəməzəməc ʦauʦau, saka nu maqadaw qacuvi.
S.FORM: saka nu qezemezemetj caucau, saka nu maqadaw qatjuvi.
S.PHON: saka nu qəzəməzəməʦ ʦauʦau, saka nu maqadaw qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: At night it was human, and in the day it was a snake.

S.FORM: inika tja ɫengɫengan a zuma.
S.PHON: inika ca ʎəŋʎəŋan a zuma.
S.FORM: inika tja ljengljengan a zuma.
S.PHON: inika ʦa ʎəŋʎəŋan a zuma.
S.TRANSL: The rest of us couldn’t see it.

S.FORM: izuazua tua vakar a pinaseleman tua itung.
S.PHON: izuazua tua vakar a pinasəɭəman tua ituŋ.
S.FORM: izuazua tua vakar a pinaseleman tua itung.
S.PHON: izuazua tua vakaR a pinasəɣəman tua ituŋ.
S.TRANSL: It was in a bamboo basket hidden by clothes.

S.FORM: saka inika ui tja patsunan ni Kului.
S.PHON: saka inika ui ca paʦunan ni kuɭui.
S.FORM: saka inika ui tja pacunan ni Kului.
S.PHON: saka inika ui ʦa paʦunan ni kuɣui.
S.TRANSL: Kului didn’t allow us to look at it.

S.FORM: manu "ki anema izua?" aya ti sa Ulung a kaka.
S.PHON: manu "ki anəma izua" aja ti sa uɭuŋ a kaka.
S.FORM: manu "ki anema izua?" aya ti sa Ulung a kaka.
S.PHON: manu "ki anəma izua" aja ti sa uɣuŋ a kaka.
S.TRANSL: “What’s in there?” said her sibling Ulung.

S.FORM: "aku sikaqiɫi aya tja qayamen?"
S.PHON: "aku sikaqiʎi aja ca qajamən"
S.FORM: "aku sikaqilji aya tja qayamen?"
S.PHON: "aku sikaqiʎi aja ʦa qajamən"
S.TRANSL: “Why are we not allowed to see it?”

S.FORM: "sa-u satjatjanu," ayain ti Kului ni sa Ulung.
S.PHON: "sa-u sacacanu," ajain ti kuɭui ni sa uɭuŋ.
S.FORM: "sa-u satjatjanu," ayain ti Kului ni sa Ulung.
S.PHON: "sa-u saʦaʦanu," ajain ti kuɣui ni sa uɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: “Go to the spring for water,” said Ulung to Kului.

S.FORM: ka qayamen, manu qatjuvi a vuluvulung.
S.PHON: ka qajamən, manu qacuvi a vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: ka qayamen, manu qatjuvi a vuluvulung.
S.PHON: ka qajamən, manu qaʦuvi a vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: When (s)he had a look, it was a hundred-pace snake.

S.FORM: saka "aku ka qayamen aken?" aya, sa vaik anga zazua malipat (tua) valuku a i navaɫ.
S.PHON: saka "aku ka qajamən akən" aja, sa vaik aŋa zazua maɭipat (tua) vaɭuku a i navaʎ.
S.FORM: saka "aku ka qayamen aken?" aya, sa vaik anga zazua malipat (tua) valuku a i navalj.
S.PHON: saka "aku ka qajamən akən" aja, sa vaik aŋa zazua maɣipat (tua) vaɣuku a i navaʎ.
S.TRANSL: “Why are you looking at me?” it said, and disappeared down the hole in the south.

S.FORM: ka mangtjez ti Kului kasi tjatjan, ka qayamen a vakar, nekanga a qatjuvi.
S.PHON: ka maŋcəz ti kuɭui kasi cacan, ka qajamən a vakar, nəkaŋa a qacuvi.
S.FORM: ka mangtjez ti Kului kasi tjatjan, ka qayamen a vakar, nekanga a qatjuvi.
S.PHON: ka maŋʦəz ti kuɣui kasi ʦaʦan, ka qajamən a vakaR, nəkaŋa a qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: When Kului came back from the spring and looked at the basket, there was no snake.

S.FORM: "inu anga?" aya ti Kului.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa" aja ti kuɭui.
S.FORM: "inu anga?" aya ti Kului.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa" aja ti kuɣui.

S.FORM: "aku su pinivakar a qatjuvi?" aya ti sa Ulung, sa kagarangi ti Kului.
S.PHON: "aku su pinivakar a qacuvi" aja ti sa uɭuŋ, sa kagaraŋi ti kuɭui.
S.FORM: "aku su pinivakar a qatjuvi?" aya ti sa Ulung, sa kagarangi ti Kului.
S.PHON: "aku su pinivakaR a qaʦuvi" aja ti sa uɣuŋ, sa kagaRaŋi ti kuɣui.
S.TRANSL: “Why did you put a snake in the basket?” said Ulung, and scolded Kului.

S.FORM: manu "ku zukatj azua," aya ti sa Kului.
S.PHON: manu "ku zukac azua," aja ti sa kuɭui.
S.FORM: manu "ku zukatj azua," aya ti sa Kului.
S.PHON: manu "ku zukaʦ azua," aja ti sa kuɣui.

S.FORM: "ki maya nu ranaven tu vaik amen, ka nu qayamen.
S.PHON: "ki maja nu ranavən tu vaik amən, ka nu qajamən.
S.FORM: "ki maya nu ranaven tu vaik amen, ka nu qayamen.
S.PHON: "ki maja nu Ranavən tu vaik amən, ka nu qajamən.
S.TRANSL: “I suppose you deliberately made us go, when you had a look.

S.FORM: ɫakua ki tja kudain? vaik aken a kitsiur," aya.
S.PHON: ʎakua ki ca kudainʔ vaik akən a kiʦiur," aja.
S.FORM: ljakua ki tja kudain' vaik aken a kiciur," aya.
S.PHON: ʎakua ki ʦa kudainʔ vaik akən a kiʦiuR," aja.
S.TRANSL: What are we to do? I’m going off with him,” she said.

S.FORM: saka maulaw anga, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: saka mauɭaw aŋa, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: saka maulaw anga, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: saka mauɣaw aŋa, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: And she disappeared, according to the story.
